Automates closing of stale pull requests using GitHub Actions with a 30-day inactivity threshold.

# Purpose
The configuration file defines a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically manages stale pull requests in a repository. It schedules the workflow to run daily at midnight using a cron expression. The workflow requires write permissions for pull requests and executes a job named `stale` on an `ubuntu-24.04` runner. The job uses the `actions/stale@v9` action to identify pull requests that have been inactive for more than 30 days, marking them with a `stale` label and posting a message to notify contributors. If no further activity occurs, the workflow will automatically close the stale pull requests after 7 days, although they can be reopened at any time.
